verbGlossary.AUTHORS: {source} produces or is the author of record for {target} documents (e.g., architect authors specification sections that govern construction quality)
verbGlossary.DRAFTS PER: {source} writes project documents structured according to {target} system (e.g., specifications writer drafts sections organized by MasterFormat divisions)
verbGlossary.DESIGNS TO: {source}'s engineering deliverables must satisfy {target} structural or performance requirements (e.g., structural engineer designs to IBC load and seismic provisions)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES BY: {source} tags or categorizes its deliverables using {target} taxonomy (e.g., MEP engineer classifies systems by UniFormat element groups)
verbGlossary.CERTIFIES UNDER: {source} stamps or certifies that its design or installation meets {target} requirements (e.g., fire protection engineer certifies suppression system design under NFPA standards)
verbGlossary.INTERPRETS: {source} provides expert interpretation of {target} for project-specific application (e.g., code consultant interprets IBC occupancy and egress requirements for permit review)
verbGlossary.ADMINISTERS: {source} manages the project's compliance program and documentation for the target certification (e.g., LEED consultant administers credit tracking and submittal packages for LEED certification)
verbGlossary.VERIFIES AGAINST: {source} performs analysis or testing to confirm assemblies meet {target} performance thresholds (e.g., building envelope consultant verifies wall assemblies against energy code U-value requirements)
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES BY: {source} establishes the project's cost or schedule breakdown using {target} hierarchy (e.g., GC structures project budget and schedule using WBS codes)
verbGlossary.BUDGETS UNDER: {source} allocates project funds according to {target} classification structure (e.g., GC sets up budget line items organized under the company's budget code structure)
verbGlossary.INVOICES AGAINST: {source} submits pay applications or cost charges referencing {target} code (e.g., subcontractor invoices labor and material against assigned standard cost codes)
verbGlossary.SCOPED UNDER: {source}'s contract scope is defined by {target} divisions or sections (e.g., drywall subcontractor is scoped under MasterFormat Division 09)
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source}inspects, cites violations, and ensures compliance with {target} or standard (e.g., building department enforces IBC provisions during plan review and field inspection)
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source}requires organizations to follow {target} reporting and documentation rules (e.g., OSHA mandates injury and illness recordkeeping per OSHA 300 log requirements)
verbGlossary.TESTS PER: {source} performs material or system tests following {target} acceptance criteria and methods (e.g., materials testing lab tests concrete cylinders per specification section requirements)
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONS FOR: {source} performs functional performance testing to satisfy the target certification's commissioning prerequisites (e.g., CxA commissions HVAC systems for LEED Energy & Atmosphere credits)
verbGlossary.EXECUTES UNDER: {source} enters into and performs work governed by {target} standard's terms and conditions (e.g., design-builder executes project delivery under AIA contract documents)
verbGlossary.OPERATES UNDER: {source}'s project role and risk allocation is defined by {target} (e.g., CM at Risk operates under a GMP delivery method classification)
verbGlossary.FABRICATES PER: {source} manufactures components tagged and dimensioned according to {target} object schema (e.g., steel fabricator produces members tagged per IFC structural element classes)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source}-level metric is broken down by {target} for comparative analysis (e.g., subcontractor safety rate segmented by safety classification categories)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source}-level metric is compared against {target} adoption or completeness threshold (e.g., trade coverage rate benchmarked to WBS adoption rate across portfolio)
